summary: Imam Watheq discusses the miracles (Mu'jizat) of the Quran and compares them with miracles of other prophets. He explains that prophets are given the highest level of miracles from Allah to supersede the accomplishments of their time periods. The speaker provides examples: Prophet Moses was given the staff that turned into a serpent to overcome the magicians of his era, and Prophet Jesus was given miracles of healing the blind and raising the dead to surpass the medical knowledge of his time. The central focus is on Prophet Muhammad's miracle being the Quran itself - a book revealed to an illiterate man that not only contained eloquent speech surpassing the Arab linguistic excellence of that era, but also contained scientific facts about the rotation of the sun and other details unknown at the time of revelation.
suggested_title: The Miracles of the Quran: Divine Signs Through the Prophets
catchy_title: Why Was the Quran Prophet Muhammad's Greatest Miracle?
